original: St. Eusebius begins his reign as Catholic Pope
Bolesław I the Brave is crowned in Gniezno, becoming the first King of Poland
Bona Sforza is crowned queen consort of Poland
Maurice of Saxony occupies the city of Linz
Osman declares war on Austria
Sudbury, Massachusetts, attacked by Indians
"Germantown Quaker Petition Against Slavery": Francis Daniel Pastorius presents 1st formal written protest against African-American slavery in English colonies in Germantown, Pennsylvania
Real Academia de la Historia ("Royal Academy of History") founded in Madrid
Paul Revere, William Dawes and Samuel Prescott ride from Charlestown to Lexington warning "the regulars are coming out!"
France & Austria sign ceasefire
First run of 2,000 guineas horse race at Newmarket, England
Whaling ship the Manhattan captained by Mercator Cooper is the first ship officially permitted visit Edo, Japan, in 220 years rescuing shipwrecked sailors
American victory at the battle of Cerro Gordo opens the way for invasion of Mexico
Russian Chancellor Count Nesselrode resigns
Battle of Fort Jackson, Fort St Philip & New Orlean's, Louisiana
Battle of Dybbøl: Denmark is defeated by Prussian and Austrian forces storming Dybbol fort in the deciding battle of the Second Schleswig War
Battle of Poison Springs, Arkansas (Camden Expedition)
San Francisco Society for Prevention of Cruelty to Animals forms in San Francisco, California
1st international cricket match, held in San Francisco, won by California
Daniel O'Leary completes a 500 mile walk in 139 hrs 32 min
Trial of Standing Bear-Crook on Native American citizenship rights begins
F4 tornado strikes Marshfield in Missouri, killing 99 people and injuring 100
Natural History Museum opens in South Kensington, England
NY Commission of Emigration ends, closing Castle Garden, NYC (now known as Castle Clinton)
John McGraw, at 26, has his managerial debut as Oriole manager
L'Humanité, under Jean Jaurès, begins publication
The Los Angeles Times story on the Azusa Street Revival launches Pentecostalism as a worldwide religious movement
Augustus Thomas' "Witching Hour" premieres in NYC
Fairmont Hotel opens in San Francisco
Cunard liner RMS Carpathia brings 705 survivors from the RMS Titanic to New York City
Tripolitan War: in continuing hostilities, Turkey closes the Dardanelles Straits after an Italian naval bombardment of the coastline
French pilot Roland Garros is shot down and glides to a landing on the German side of the lines during World War I
US Secretary of State Warns Germany that the USA may break diplomatic relations unless torpedo attacks on unarmed ships stop
The People's Council of Latvia proclaims the independence of the new country of Latvia
Junior Achievement incorporated in Colorado Springs
Philip James Barry's "Punch for Judy" premieres in NYC
Netherlands soccer team defeats Denmark 2-0
74,000 (62,281 paid) on hand for opening of Yankee Stadium
Poland annexes Central Lithuania
1st crossword puzzle book published by Simon & Schuster
World's Fair opens in Chicago
Rhein Stadium opens in Dusseldorf, Germany
Palace for People's industry in Amsterdam devastated by fire
Attempted raid on the armoury of police and auxiliary forces in Chittagong in Bengal province, British India by armed pro-independence revolutionaries led by Surya Sen popularly known as Master-da
BBC news announcer announces "there is no news" at 20:45 news bulletin, plays music instead
C.A. Tannahill opens 1st US "Washateria" (laundromat) in Fort Worth, Texas; four electric washing machines available for hourly rental - no dryers were available
Gen Sarazen's double eagle on 15th, wins him his 2nd Masters
Netherlands election (Musserts NSB wins 8% of vote)
Pan-Am Clipper begins regular passenger flights from San Francisco to Honolulu
Headless Mad Butcher victim found in Cleveland
Superman first appears in DC Comics' Action Comics Series Issue #1, dated June 1938
Hubert Pierlot forms Belgian government
"Stars & Stripes" paper for US armed forces starts
Stanley Cup Final, Maple Leaf Gardens, Toronto, ON: Toronto Maple Leafs beat Detroit Red Wings, 3-1 for a 4-3 series win; Toronto recovers after losing first 3 games
1 armed outfielder, St Louis Brown Pete Gray, 1st game he goes 1 for 4
Clandestine Radio 1212, after broadcasting pro-nazi propaganda for months used their influence to trap 350,000 German army group B troops
Diplomatic relations between the Soviet Union and Bolivia established
Epe freed (by corporal G van Aken)
International Court of Justice opens at The Hague Netherlands
League of Nations dissolves (3 months after UN starts)
Republic of Ireland withdraws from British Commonwealth
1st opening night-game, Cards beat Pirates, 4-2
Polish Catholic church & government sign accord over relations
Sam Jethroe is 1st African American to play for Boston Braves
Yankees win 15-10 after trailing Red Sox 9-0 in 6th
Dutch Antilles government of Da Costa Gomez forms
France, West Germany & Benelux form European Steel & Coal Community
1st "Walk"/"Don't Walk" lighted street signals installed
1st Bandoeng Conference opens in Indonesia, attended by 29 nations - first international assembly of Asian-African nations
Egypt & Israel agree to a cease fire
Government troops reconquer Padang, Middle-Sumatra, Indonesia
NL single-game record crowd of 78,682 as the Los Angeles Dodgers win their first ever home game over San Francisco Giants, 6-5, at Los Angeles Memorial Coliseum
Stanley Cup Final, Montreal Forum, Montreal, Quebec: Montreal Canadiens' 4th straight title; beat Toronto Maple Leafs, 5-3 for a 4-1 series win
CONCP is founded in Casablanca as a united front of African movements opposing Portuguese colonial rule
Dr James Campbell performed the 1st human nerve transplant
Stanley Cup Final, Maple Leaf Gardens, Toronto, ON: Toronto Maple Leafs go back-to-back with a 3-1 Game 5 win over Detroit Red Wings for a 4-1 series victory
Artisans strike in Belgium ends
Jonathan Frid makes his 1st appearance as 200-year-old vampire Barnabas Collins on TV's "Dark Shadows", show becomes a huge hit
178,000 employees of US Bell Telephone System go on strike
Dutch Department of Amnesty International forms
London Bridge is sold to US oil company (to be erected in Arizona)
Mart Crowley's "Boys in the Band" premieres in NYC
Peter Luke's "Hadrian VII" premieres in London
San Francisco's Old Hall of Justice demolished
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Greek actress and activist Melina Mercouri establishes Greek Aid Fund
The Widgery Report on 'Bloody Sunday' in Northern Ireland is published, causing outrage among the people of Derry who call it the "Widgery Whitewash"
Red Brigade kidnaps Italian attorney general Mario Sossi
30th Tony Awards: "Travesties" (play) & "A Chorus Line" (musical) win
Pulitzer prize awarded to Michael Cristofer for his play "Shadow Box"
Senate votes to turn Panama Canal over to Panama on Dec 31, 1999
"Real People" premieres on NBC TV
Major Haddad declares South-Lebanon independent
Zimbabwe (formerly Southern Rhodesia) declares independence from the United Kingdom
Longest game in Professional Baseball: Pawtucket Red Sox tie Rochester Red Wings 2-2 in 32 innings (game resumed 23rd June)
Atlanta Braves win record 11th straight opening game (beat Astros)
Canada Constitution Act replaces British North America Act
Zimbabwean capital Salisbury renamed Harare
A lone suicide bomber kills 63, at US Embassy in Lebanon
KMO-AM in Tacoma Wash changes call letters to KAMT (now KKMO)
Joan Benoit runs world record female marathon (2:22:43)
Space Shuttle Challenger flies back to Kennedy Space Center via Kelly Air Force Base
"Wham!" become the first western pop act to release an album in China
IBM produces 1st megabit-chip
Robert M Gates becomes deputy director of CIA
Titan rocket explodes seconds after liftoff from Vandenberg AFB
Bettino Craxi resigns as Prime Minister of Italy
Bob Land wins his 6th straight Kenduskeag Stream Canoe Race
Gregory Robertson does 200-mph free fall to save unconscious skydiver near Coolidge, Arizona
Pat Knauff, France sets 1-leg downhill ski speed record (115.012 mph)
Bankruptcy court forces Frank Lorenzo to give up Eastern Airlines
Birmingham Fire issued an original franchise in WLAF
Supreme Court rules states could make it a crime to possess or look at child pornography, even in one's home
Congress ends railroad worker 1 day strike
US Census Bureau said it failed to count up to 63 million in 1990 census
Start of South Africa's 1st Test Cricket since 1970 (v WI Bridgetown)
Lebanon drops relations with Iran
STS-59 (Endeavour) lands [approx]
Houston Post folds after 116 years
In Lebanon, at least 106 civilians are killed when the Israel Defense Forces accidentally shell the UN compound at Quana.
The Supreme Court of the United States upholds the Partial-Birth Abortion Ban Act in a 5-4 decision
NBA owners give approval of a potential Seattle SuperSonics' relocation to Oklahoma City in a 28–2 vote by the Board of Governors
The Casoria Contemporary Art Museum in Naples begins burning artworks after cultural institution budget cuts
27 people are killed and 65 are injured in a cafe bombing in Baghdad, Iraq
Two earth-like planets are discovered orbiting the star Kepler-62
12 Nepalese climbers are killed by an avalanche on Mt Everest
British Prime Minister Teresa May announces she will seek a "snap" election
Protests begin in Managua, Nicaragua, over proposed changes to social security, protesters beaten by suspected pro-government gangs
“Black Panther” is the first film shown at a commercial cinema in 35 years in Saudi Arabia as cinemas are reopened
Irish Journalist Lyra McKee shot to death covering riots in Derry, Northern Ireland with dissident republican group the New IRA claiming responsibility
Canada's worst modern mass shooting as a gunman kills 18 people, including a Royal Canadian Mounted Police officer, across Nova Scotia
French flagship aircraft carrier Charles de Gaulle reports over 1,000 cases of COVID-19, prompting an investigation
12-team European Super League is announced to widespread condemnation amongst governments, non-involved clubs, press and fans; amidst the furore all 6 EPL clubs withdraw within 3 days
Russia begins a major new offensive in Donbas, during their invasion of Ukraine
Fox News settles lawsuit with Dominion Voting Systems for $787.5 million, moments before trial, with Fox admitting it had defamed Dominion during US 2020 election by broadcasting conspiracy theories
Fossil evidence of the largest known snake 11-15 meters long named Vasuki indicus, 66 million years old, published from a mine in Panandhro, western India
New report finds nearly half of China's cities are sinking due to development and extracting too much water
Police are sent in to remove students on campus at Columbia University protesting Israeli actions in Gaza - ignites protests across US campuses
United States vetoes a United Nations Security Council resolution recognising Palestine as a state with the UK and Switzerland abstaining
Diet of Worms: Cardinal Alexander questionsMartin Luther
William Shakespeare's poem "Venus and Adonis" is registered for publication - his first work to be printed and his most famous work during his lifetime
Dutch Republic underJohan de Wittand Bishop von Galen of Münster sign the Treaty of Kleve
Paul Reveremeets withSamuel AdamsandJohn Hancockin Lexington, Massachusetts, and warns them that British troops are marching from Boston to Lexington

George Washingtonissues General Orders announcing the end of hostilities with Britain in the American Revolutionary War, giving thanks to the Almighty, offering congratulations, and authorizing an extra ration of alcohol to the troops to celebrate
William Lamb(Lord Melbourne) becomes Prime Minister of the United Kingdom afterRobert Peelresigns out of frustration
ColonelRobert E. Leeturns down offer to command Union armies (US Civil War)
Confederate GeneralJoseph E. Johnstonsurrenders to GeneralWilliam T. Shermanin North Carolina
David Livingstone, African explorer, buried in Westminster Abbey
St. Andrew's Ambulance Association is granted a Royal Charter byQueen Victoria
Canadian championTommy BurnsKOs Jewey Smith of England in 5th round at Neuilly Bowling Palace, Paris to retain his world heavyweight boxing title
Edith Whartonis appointed Chevalier of the Legion of Honour, France’s highest award, for her contribution to the war effort
Cleveland center fielderTris Speakerturns an unassisted double play
Nationalist leaderChiang Kai-Shekforms an anti-government in China
Adolf HitlernamesJoachim von Ribbentropambassador for disarmament
Franz von Papenbecomes German ambassador in Turkey
The Doolittle Raid:James Doolittleleads the first air raid on mainland Japan, bombing Tokyo & other Japanese cities
Leonard BernsteinandJerome Robbins' ballet "Fancy Free" premieres at the Metropolitan Opera House, NYC
Jackie Robinsondebuts as 2nd baseman for the Montreal Royals
US recognizesTito's Yugoslavia government
"New Orleans", an American musical romance film premieres;Louis ArmstrongandBillie Holiday, in her only feature film, play supporting roles
Egyptian ColonelGamal Abdel Nasserseizes power and appoints himself Prime Minister
A United States federal court rules that poetEzra Poundis to be released from an insane asylum.
LA Dodgers pitcherSandy Koufaxstrikes out the side on 9 pitches, for major league best third time in his career
Bill Russellbecomes first African American coach in NBA history (Boston Celtics)
US Government ends Mandatory Oil Import Program, established in 1959 by PresidentEisenhower

John Lennongives what becomes his final public performance at a gala salute to British media mogul Lew Grade at the Grand Ballroom of the Hilton Hotel, NYC; he performed 3 songs -Little Richard’s “Slippin’ and Slidin’”, Ben E. King’s “Stand By Me”, and closed with his own “Imagine”
Alex Haleyauthor of "Roots" awarded Pulitzer Prize
Pulitzer Prize awarded toAlice Walkerfor the novel "The Color Purple"
Mike Schmidthits 500th home run (vs Robinson-Pirates)
Barbra Streisandrecords "Warm All Over"
John Stocktonbreaks his own NBA season assist record at 1,136
Arsenio Hallannounces he will end his late-night TV talk show in May 1994
CricketerBrian Larahits 375 runs on 1 day for WI vs England to beatGarfield Sobers' world record
Former US PresidentRichard Nixonsuffers a stroke and passes away 4 days later
QuarterbackJoe Montanaannounces his retirement from football
NFL Draft: Tennessee quarterbackPeyton Manningfirst pick by Indianapolis Colts
Wayne Gretzkyplays his last game in the NHL, as his New York Rangers lose 2-1 to the Pittsburgh Penguins
Jason Koumasscores a last minute goal in a 1-0 win against Sunderland
"Iron Man 3", directed by Shane Black, starringRobert Downey Jr. andGwyneth Paltrow, premieres in London
28th Rock and Roll Hall of Fame inductees: Heart; Albert King; Randy Newman; Public Enemy; Rush;Donna Summer;Quincy Jones; and Lou Adler
Marco van Bastenis announced to replace Dick Advocaat as the manager of AZ Alkmaar at the start of the 2014-15 season

30th Rock and Roll Hall of Fame inductees: The Paul Butterfield Blues Band;Green Day; Joan Jett and the Blackhearts; Lou Reed;Stevie Ray Vaughanand Double Trouble;Bill Withers; The "5" Royals; andRingo Starr
Kawhi Leonardwins the NBA Defensive Player of the Year for the second consecutive year
Laureus World Sports Awards, Palais am Funkturm, Berlin, Germany: Sportsman:Novak Đoković; Sportswoman: Sernea Williams; Team: New Zealand Men's National Rugby Union team
Portuguese superstarCristiano Ronaldobecomes first player to score 100 goals in the Champions League with a hat-trick in Real Madrid's 4-2 win over Bayern Munich

"Together At Home" virtual benefit concert for the World Health Organization's COVID-19 Solidarity Response Fund curated byLady Gaga; 6-hour pre-show streams on-line, followed by 2-hour global telecast; performers include: Andra Day, Common, The Killers,Charlie Puth,John Legend,Jennifer Hudson,Annie Lennox, and Zucchero
South African PresidentCyril Ramaphosadeclares a national state of disaster, after floods and mudslides in Durban area result in over 400 deaths and destroy over 4,000 homes
US Federal judge rules as "unlawful"Bidenadministration’s mandate that masks be worn on public transportation
The cornerstone of the current St. Peter's Basilica is laid in the Vatican by PopeJulius II
San Francisco earthquake and fire kills nearly 4,000 while destroying 75% of the city
Joan of Arcreceives beatification by the Roman Catholic Church at St. Peter's Basilica in the Vatican
